node.statement: (Injectivity of Φ). The map T ↦ ω_T is injective. Proof: Suppose ω_{T_1} = ω_{T_2}. Then for all a ∈ A, ⟨π_φ(a)(T_1 - T_2)ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 0. Since ξ_φ is cyclic for π_φ, the set {π_φ(a)ξ_φ : a ∈ A} is dense in H_φ. Setting S = T_1 - T_2 ∈ π_φ(A)' (self-adjoint), we have ⟨Sπ_φ(a)ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 0 for all a (using S ∈ commutant to move S past π_φ(a)). Then ⟨Sξ_φ, π_φ(a)*ξ_φ⟩ = 0 for all a, i.e., Sξ_φ ⊥ π_φ(A)*ξ_φ = π_φ(A)ξ_φ, which is dense. So Sξ_φ = 0. Now for any b ∈ A: Sπ_φ(b)ξ_φ = π_φ(b)Sξ_φ = 0 (since S commutes with π_φ). By density of π_φ(A)ξ_φ and continuity of S, S = 0, so T_1 = T_2.
